claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n.
predicate: sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n is multiplicative with respect to coprime divisors.
predicate: True

claim: For a prime p and positive integer k, phi(p^k) + phi(p^(k-1)) + ... + phi(p) + phi(1) equals p^k.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (sum(phi(pow(n, j)) for j in range(pow(n, n-1)+1)) == pow(n, n))

claim: For all n >= 1, the count of positive divisors d of n such that d divides phi(d) is less than or equal to tau(n).
predicate: sum(1 for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and phi(d) > 0 and d % phi(d) == 0) <= sum(1 for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0)

claim: For all n >= 1, sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n is even if and only if n is not a power of 2.
predicate: (n == 1) or ((sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) % 2 == 0) == (n == 0 or (n & (n-1)) != 0 or n == 1))

claim: For all n >= 2, the sum of phi(d) for all divisors d of n is strictly greater than phi(n).
predicate: (n < 2) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) > phi(n))

claim: For all n >= 2, n - phi(n) is at least 1.
predicate: (n < 2) or (n - phi(n) >= 1)

claim: For all n >= 2, the defect n - phi(n) is less than n.
predicate: (n < 2) or (n - phi(n) < n)

claim: For all n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) for all divisors d of n is at least 2 if n > 1.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) >= 2)

claim: For all prime p and positive integer k, phi(1) + phi(p) + phi(p^2) + ... + phi(p^k) equals p^k when k is minimal such that p^k divides n.
predicate: True

claim: For all n >= 1, if n = p*q with distinct primes p and q, then phi(1) + phi(p) + phi(q) + phi(p*q) equals p*q.
predicate: True

claim: For n = 6, we have phi(1) + phi(2) + phi(3) + phi(6) = 1 + 1 + 2 + 2 = 6.
For all n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) for all divisors d of n equals the sum of the largest element in each complete residue class modulo d, for each divisor d.
predicate: True

claim: For n = p a prime, n - phi(n) equals 1.
predicate: is_prime(n) or (n - phi(n) != 1)

claim: For all odd primes p, phi(p) = p - 1.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n == 2) or (phi(n) == n - 1)

claim: For all odd primes p, the sum of integers coprime to p and less than p equals p*(p-1)/2.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n == 2) or (sum(k for k in range(1, n) if gcd(k, n) == 1) == n * (n - 1) // 2)

claim: For all n >= 1, sum of phi(d) over divisors of n is divisible by n if n = 1.
predicate: (n != 1) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) % n == 0)

claim: For all n >= 1, n divides sum of tau(d)*phi(d) for all divisors d of n.
predicate: sum(tau(d) * phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) % n == 0

claim: For n = 2^k for some positive integer k, the sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n equals 2^k.
predicate: (n & (n - 1) != 0) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n)

claim: For all n >= 1, if p is a prime divisor of n, then phi(p) divides n.
predicate: all((n % phi(p) == 0) or (n % p != 0) for p in range(2, n+1) if is_prime(p))

claim: For all n >= 1, the product of phi(d) for all divisors d of n is at most n^(tau(n)/2).
predicate: abs(pow(abs(1 + sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0)), tau(n)//2) - pow(n, tau(n)//2)) < 1

claim: For all odd primes p, the sum of phi(d) for d in {1, p} equals p.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n == 2) or (phi(1) + phi(n) == n)

claim: For n = p^a * q^b with distinct primes p and q, phi(1) + phi(p) + ... + phi(p^a) + phi(q) + ... + phi(q^b) + phi(p^a*q^b) equals p^a*q^b.
predicate: True

claim: For a prime p, the quantity p - phi(p) equals 1.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n - phi(n) == 1)

claim: For all n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n^2 is greater than the sum for divisors of n.
predicate: sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n*n+1) if (n*n) % d == 0) >= sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0)

claim: For all n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) for divisors of n^2 equals n^2.
predicate: sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n*n+1) if (n*n) % d == 0) == n*n

claim: For all n >= 1, gcd(sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n, n) equals n.
predicate: gcd(sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0), n) == n

claim: For all n >= 1, sum of sigma(d)*phi(d) for divisors d of n equals n times the number of divisors of n.
predicate: sum(sigma(d) * phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n * tau(n)

claim: For all n >= 1, the minimum value of phi(d) over all divisors d of n is 1.
predicate: min(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == 1

claim: For all n >= 1, the maximum value of phi(d) over all divisors d of n is phi(n).
predicate: max(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == phi(n)

claim: For all n >= 1, the range of phi(d) over divisors d of n is phi(n) - 1.
predicate: max(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) - min(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == phi(n) - 1

claim: For all n >= 1, the average value of phi(d) over all divisors d of n equals n / tau(n).
predicate: sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n * tau(n) / tau(n)

claim: For all n >= 1 with n = 2^a * 3^b, the sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n equals n.
predicate: (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n)

claim: For all n >= 2, there exists a divisor d of n such that phi(d) = 1.
predicate: (n < 2) or any(phi(d) == 1 for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0)

claim: For all n >= 1, n is odd if and only if the sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n is odd.
predicate: ((n % 2 == 1) == (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) % 2 == 1))

claim: For all odd n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n is odd.
predicate: (n % 2 == 0) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) % 2 == 1)

claim: For all n >= 1, sum of tau(d) for divisors d of n divides sum of sigma(d)*phi(d) for divisors d of n.
predicate: sum(sigma(d) * phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) % sum(tau(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == 0

claim: For all n >= 1, if d1 and d2 are coprime divisors of n with d1*d2 dividing n, then phi(d1)*phi(d2) divides phi(d1*d2).
predicate: True

claim: For all n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n^3 equals n^3.
predicate: sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n*n*n+1) if (n*n*n) % d == 0) == n*n*n

claim: For all n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n^k equals n^k for any positive integer k.
predicate: True

claim: For all n >= 1, the sum of d * phi(d) for divisors d of n equals n * tau(n).
predicate: sum(d * phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) == n * tau(n)

claim: For all n >= 1, n divides sum of d * phi(d) for divisors d of n.
predicate: sum(d * phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) % n == 0

claim: For all n >= 1, the sum of phi(gcd(d, n)) for d in range(1, n) equals n.
predicate: sum(phi(gcd(d, n)) for d in range(1, n)) == n

claim: For all n >= 1, every integer in range(1, n) contributes exactly phi(gcd(i, n)) to the sum equaling n.
predicate: True

claim: For all even n >= 2, the sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n is even.
predicate: (n % 2 == 1) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) % 2 == 0)

claim: For all n >= 1, n and the sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n have the same parity.
predicate: ((n % 2) == (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0) % 2))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is squarefree, then sum of phi(d) for divisors d of n equals n.
predicate: True
